title: linaria polygalifolia subsp. aguillonensis
intro: Linaria polygalifolia subsp. aguillonensis, also known as Aguillon's toadflax, is a plant species belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Linaria polygalifolia subsp. aguillonensis include its ability to relieve congestion, reduce fever, and soothe sore throats. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of its leaves and flowers, which can be used as teas, tinctures, or added to other herbal remedies.
